programmes.show_description: Soup, Salad, And Seafood. Guy Fieri dives into classic comfort foods in Kansas City, MO. He visits a chef serving up chicken pot pie and steamed mussels, and food truck-turned deli. S18, Ep13
Tacos And Tots. Guy Fieri visits joints taking childhood favourites to a gourmet level. They include a Denver bar cooking up tater tots, pork rinds and a pastrami sandwich. S17, Ep1
Tampa Bay. Adam is on the hunt for authentic flavours and delicious dishes in Tampa, Florida. Later, he recruits a pro-wrestler to take down a scorching hot platter of wings. S1, Ep3
New Orleans, LA. Casey Webb heads to New Orleans for the best of the Big Easy, including a pound sundae with eight large scoops of ice cream and smothered with eight toppings. S1, Ep6
Ships Ahoy, Mary's Mix-Up And Elevator Error. It's Fleet Week in New York and Navy ships are in port. The bakery creates an amazing cake to honour the Navy's service men and women. S9, Ep25
Father's Day Collapse, Buddy's Surprise, And Tee Time. The crew makes a basketball-inspired cake as a Father's Day surprise for a hoop-mad dad. But disaster strikes during delivery! S9, Ep26

Rick Stein makes the popular steak and kidney pudding as well as poached pears with mascarpone ice cream. Plus, he visits a walled garden in East Sussex. S1, Ep5
Rick Stein forages for golden chanterelles and mushrooms in Norfolk, for a kidney breakfast dish. Plus, he tries charcuterie for the first time. S1, Ep6
Tyne And Wear/Newcastle. Si takes Dave around his beloved home turf of Tyne and Wear, and Newcastle. They visit the Angel of the North, and a bakery that makes delicious stotties. S1, Ep8
Faster Pasta. Ree shares her quick, delicious and crowd-pleasing pasta recipes. She makes lemon pasta, grilled steak pasta and drunken spaghetti with red wine. S22, Ep6
No Cooking Required. Ree shares some of her favourite cooking-free recipes. She adds a cowboy spin to her ranch muffuletta sandwich, Chinese chicken salad and ice cream. S22, Ep7
Jamie cooks up his version of steak and onion sandwich and shows his way to cook osso buco. Plus, he makes a sticky toffee pudding with his mum. S1, Ep3
Jamie serves up three comfort food classics: chicken kiev, shepherd's pie and spaghetti alle vongole with a non-traditional pink twist. S1, Ep4
Library Inaugural Drinks. Ina makes fig and fennel caponata, an antipasto platter and mashed chickpeas and mussels to thank the team that designed and built a library at her barn. S8, Ep6
Perfect Day In East Hampton. Ina whips up raspberry muffins for breakfast. Later, she cooks with the local chef Joe Isidori before heading back to the barn for soup with Jeffrey. S8, Ep7
Cake. The 12 new bakers don their aprons and head for the iconic tent for cake week, starting with the signature challenge, to make the well-loved Madeira cake. S6, Ep1
Biscuits. The remaining home bakers try to prove their skill at making 24 biscotti, and they tackle labour-intensive Arlettes in a tricky technical challenge. S6, Ep2
Blair Castle. James's culinary tour continues in Scotland with a trip to Blair Castle in the Highlands, where he's joined by top Michelin-starred chef Tom Kitchin. S1, Ep3
Edinburgh. James visits a whisky distillery in Pitlochry, where he makes a delicious whisky chicken dish while Tom shares his local knowledge of Edinburgh. S1, Ep4
Tyne And Wear/Newcastle. Si takes Dave around his beloved home turf of Tyne and Wear, and Newcastle. They visit the Angel of the North, and a bakery that makes delicious stotties. S1, Ep8

With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that won't break the budget. S1, Ep3
Jamie cooks a curried fish pie and prepares light butter and sage gnudi - gnocchi-like dumplings that are made with ricotta. Plus, he bakes a hummingbird cake. S1, Ep5
Jamie gives traditional ham, egg and chips a fiery, Jamaican twist. And, he offers a new take on chicken kebab and a tiered chocolate cake with nougat frosting. S1, Ep6
Chicken, Chili, And Chowder. Guy Fieri visits a Burbank chilli spot dating back a century, a Philadelphia gourmet bar food joint cranking out clam chowder and a Toronto smokehouse. S17, Ep2
Dynamic Duos. Guy Fieri looks at the combos making local hangouts a hit. A Denver bar smokes pork for its biscuits, and a Scottsdale duo fuses their Mexican and Mediterranean roots. S17, Ep3
Traditional Gone Wild. Guy Fieri samples dishes like mama made, if she had a wild imagination! In Philadelphia a special guest hits the kitchen for fried PB&Js and pig's head tacos. S17, Ep4
Peppers, Pork, And Poutine. Guy Fieri looks at standout veggie and meat dishes. A Phoenix chef puts her spin on a regional pork dish, and a Toronto joint serves up a Canadian staple. S17, Ep5
Portland, OR. Casey Webb heads to Portland, Oregon, to taste Peruvian chicken, a succulent porchetta sandwich and a burrito packed with ten of the world's spiciest peppers. S1, Ep7
Chicago, IL. Casey Webb travels to Chicago for some epic eats, including a huge pork sandwich and a challenge featuring a double bacon cheeseburger and 'Irish Nacho' cheese fries. S1, Ep8
Volcanoes, Drive-Ins, & Movie Magic. Buddy and the team make an erupting volcano cake for a science camp. And, there's a movie-themed cake with a projection screen inside! S9, Ep27
Farewell To Momma Mary. The guys reflect on the life of Momma Mary and her legacy. While the family grieves, they also celebrate her triumphant life and vivacious personality. S9, Ep28

With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that won't break the budget. S1, Ep3
Cajun. Si and Dave visit the hotbed of Cajun culture in Lafayette, Louisiana. There, the bikers sample crawfish and groove at a jam session. S1, Ep4
Dad's Dinner Delivery. Ree and her son, Todd, make creamy chicken cacciatore soup, Parmesan herb drop biscuits and dark chocolate cheesecake for her dad and stepmother. S22, Ep8
Ask Me Anything: Cookout. Ree answers questions from friends and Pawhuska locals. Plus, she shares her chicken fajita salad, pepperoni potato salad and southern Panzanella recipes. S22, Ep9

Game Plan. Ina Garten shares her secrets for effortless entertaining with chili tortilla chips, slow roasted beef, roasted balsamic brussels sprouts and truffled mashed potatoes. S8, Ep8
Birthday Surprise. Ina makes a surprise birthday dinner for a friend. She fills her fridge with Italian seafood salad, German chocolate cupcakes and a delicious cheeseboard. S8, Ep9
Bread. The bakers must prepare quick breads with no proving, and a deceptively simple baguette - will they impress Paul Hollywood? Plus, showstopping action with 3D bread sculptures. S6, Ep3
Desserts. The nine remaining bakers tackle creme brulee but will they be a scrambled mess? Plus, Spanische windtorte from Austria and baked cheesecake feature in the show-stopper. S6, Ep4
Yorkshire. The time has come to cross from Scotland to England, and where better to begin than James' home country of Yorkshire with chef Lisa Allen? S1, Ep5
Lancashire. James continues his adventure in Lancashire with chef Lisa Allen. They visit Lisa's hometown, Clitheroe, where they prepare a lovely John Dory dish. S1, Ep6

Palermo. Paul Hollywood visits the beautiful and historic Sicilian capital, Palermo. He explores the Arabic-inspired food markets and learns how to make perfect Cannoli. S2, Ep1
With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that won't break the budget. S1, Ep3
Kings And Queens Of 'Cue. Guy Fieri meets some masters of meat and BBQ. A Texas-inspired barbecue joint smokes pork ribs and a Denver eatery tops pizza with beer braised brisket. S17, Ep6
A Festival Of Flavour. Guy Fieri pulls over for the bounty of the county. A Denver bar creates savoury apple strudel and in Arizona a Pakistani spot serves goat and tandoori Chicken. S17, Ep7
